titleStmt.title: SII 31.71: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 71. (A.R. No. B 282 of 1938.) TIPPALŪRU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab set up in the kalam (threshing floor) at the entrance of the village.
It is dated Śaka 1430 (wrong for 1449) Sarvajit, Śrāvaṇa ba. 30, Monday corresponding to August 26, 1527 A.D.

It registers the gift of tax (Doṁbari-paṇnu) at the rate of 10 per year collected from the Dommari community of the village by Sūraparāju, the kūtari of Dombari mīsaragaṇḍa Kumāragiri Reḍi for maintaining the lamp and offerings to the temples of Iśvara and Perumāḷḷu at Tippalūru. This gift is stated to have been given for the merit of their twenty four families and the king Kṛishṇadēvarāya and the king donated Tippalūru agrahārā to the 8 poet laureates (Ashṭha diggaja Kavīśvarlu) of Kṛishṇadēvarāya court.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
Aviṣnumastu . śubhamastu . svasti
śrī . vijayābhyudaya śālivāha
na śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1430 A
gunēṁṭi sa¨rvajitu saṁva
tsara śrāvaṇa ba 30 sōmavāra
puṇyakālamaṁdu
śrīman mahārājādhi
rāja rājaparamēsvara
śrīvīrapratāpa śrīkṛṣṇa
dēva mahārāyalu mahārāyalu
Aṣṭadiggaja kavīśvarulaku dhā
ravōsi yiccina tippalūri
Agrahārānaku do
baṁrimīsaragaṁḍa ko
maragiri reḍi kūtari sū
raparājunu vīḍu vi
ḍisi tippalūri Isva

runikinni perumā
ḷa kunnu daṁḍaṁ sama
¨rpiṁci yidēvaḷaku Āra
giṁpulakunnudīpā
rādhanakunnu yi tippa
lūriki vaccina doṁ
baripannu tama 24
IruvaInālugu
kulālavāriki puṁ
ṇyaṁgāsu dhāravōsi
rāyalaku puṇyaṁgā
nu yistimi . yēṁki
yi grāmaṁvāru
l 10 lekhanu dēva
ḷaku Icuka rāgala
vāru Ani yiccina
dha¨rma śāsanaṁ . I
dha¨rmānaku yavva
ru tappināsu gaṁgaka
¨rta gōvubrāṁhma
ṇa vadhasēsina pā
pāna bōvuvāru .
tama tallitaṁḍru
lanu kāsilōnu va
dhasēsina pāpāna
bōvuvāru

tama mānābhi
ma(mā)nālu Iru
vaInalugu ku
lāla vāri gā
duyala kācē
vāniki yicina
vāru . svadattā
dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ
paradattānu pā
rattāpa
svadattaṁ
bhavēt
ha
śrī .
